title: 3 Legal Considerations
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations
3.1	Overview of Legal Considerations
Financial institutions are responsible for complying with applicable legislation and regulations. The information provided in this chapter is for convenience only and doesn芒聙聶t take the place of statutes and regulations. See the actual statute or regulation for the full text. Note that there is no guarantee of accuracy for the online statutes.
Note:	Other federal and provincial legislation may influence deposit processing (e.g., privacy legislation, human rights legislation). To address this issue, your financial institution must apply additional policies and procedures.
3.2	CPA Rules
The Canadian Payment Association (CPA), develops and implements the rules that apply to the clearing and settlement of different types of payments between its member financial institutions. See the Payments Canada website for more information on CPA Rules.
CPA Rule A1, General Rules Pertaining to Items Acceptable for Clearing, for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement
芒聙聯 Outlines the general requirements for processing Canadian dollar items and establishes the amount limit for paper items. Paper items that equal or exceed $25 million can芒聙聶t be exchanged through the clearing on the same day.
CPA Rule A3, Endorsement and Identification of Paper Items
芒聙聯 Outlines the procedures and regulations for endorsement and identification of paper items prior to processing.
CPA Rule A10, Image Rule
芒聙聯 Provides information on the use of images and image printouts (including Clearing Replacement Documents and Return Replacement Documents). An image or image printout of a cheque that is created by or on behalf of a CPA financial institution in accordance with this Rule can be used for all purposes as though it were the eligible bill. Of note for Branch Capture and Mobile Deposits is the requirement that physical cheques be destroyed within 120 days of a customer depositing the cheque.
CPA Rule G2, General Rules Pertaining to Government of Canada Paper Instruments
芒聙聯 Outlines the provisions applicable to Receiver General Warrants and other Government of Canada paper instruments.
CPA Rule G7, Verification and Collection Procedures for Receiver General Warrants
芒聙聯 Describes how to verify Receiver General Warrants with the Cheque Redemption and Control Directorate (CRCD). If the CRCD is unable to verify the authenticity of a warrant, this rule describes how to send it on collection.
CPA Rule G8, Procedures/Criteria Pertaining to the Encashment of Receiver General Warrants for Non-Customers
芒聙聯 Provides encashment and identification requirements for Receiver General Warrants for non-customers. When these procedures are followed, the federal government indemnifies financial institutions against fraud losses arising from the encashment of Receiver General Warrants up to $1,750.
CPA Rule K1, Bulk Exchange and Settlement of U.S. Dollar Paper Items
芒聙聯 Provides information on the exchange and settlement, in Canada, of US dollar items.
CPA Standards 006, Specifications for MICR-Encoded Payment Items
芒聙聯 Defines the location and background design of the necessary data elements for imageable MICR-encoded payment items and other MICR-encoded payment items.
Credit unions in BC and Ontario that subscribe to the Operations Manual Program can see the Cheques Part in The Guide for more information.
3.3	Bills of Exchange Act
The
Bills of Exchange Act
(
BEA
) governs the form of and dealings with financial instruments such as bills of exchange, cheques, and promissory notes. The
BEA
regulates financial issues such as the time permitted for the dishonour and return of cheques, forged endorsements, and the rights and powers of the bill of exchange holder.
The
BEA
states that 芒聙聹an Official Image of an eligible bill may be dealt with and used for all purposes as though it were the eligible bill芒聙聺.
3.4	Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act and Regulations (Canada)
The
PCMLTFA
and
PCMLTF Regulations
apply to financial institutions and their agents (e.g., deposit-taking agents). Contact your financial institution芒聙聶s AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
3.5	Agreements for Mobile Deposits
To participate in Mobile Deposits, your financial institution must enter into an agreement with Central 1. Your financial institution must also obtain agreement on the use of Mobile Deposits from each customer who enrolls in this service.
Financial institutions must ensure that customers agree to destroy the physical cheques submitted as mobile deposits within 120 days of the deposit as per CPA Rule A10, Image Rule. To comply with the definition of 芒聙聹Official Image芒聙聺 in the
Bills of Exchange Act
,
your financial institution芒聙聶s agreement with the customer must establish the customer芒聙聶s authority to 芒聙聹act on behalf of芒聙聺 your financial institution when creating images of cheques.
3.6	Agreement for Branch Capture Deposit Processing
Your financial institution must enter into an agreement with Central 1 before it can implement Branch Capture deposit processing. Central 1 will provide the agreement to your financial institution.
This agreement doesn芒聙聶t replace existing agreements for deposit item processing (i.e., clearing and settlement) or cheque imaging. The new agreement and the corresponding schedule deal specifically with Remote Deposit Capture Services and Branch Capture services.
3.7	Agreements for Corporate Capture Deposit Processing
To participate in Corporate Capture Deposit Processing, your financial institution must enter into an agreement with Central 1.
Your financial institution must also obtain agreement on the use of Corporate Capture Deposit Processing from each business customer who enrolls in this service. Central 1 doesn芒聙聶t provide a 芒聙聹template芒聙聺 for this agreement, as it芒聙聶s anticipated that each financial institution will want different information in their agreement with the business customer, depending on their level of risk and processes. However, Central 1 provides mandatory clauses in the Remote Deposit Capture Services Agreement 芒聙聯 Service Schedule for Corporate Remote Deposit Capture between Central 1 and your financial institution, which must be included in the agreement between your financial institution and your business customer.
Financial institutions must ensure that business customers agree to destroy the physical cheques submitted as Corporate Capture Deposit Processing within 120 days of the deposit as per CPA Rule A10, Image Rule. To comply with the definition of 芒聙聹Official Image芒聙聺 in the
Bills of Exchange Act
,
your financial institution芒聙聶s agreement with the business customer must establish the business customer芒聙聶s authority to 芒聙聹act on behalf of芒聙聺 your financial institution when creating images of cheques.
END
